name: Ocal Browser V7.7.00
body:
OCAL BROWSER - STABLE RELEASE CATALOG
Version: 7.7.00
Release Date: July 16, 2026

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INNO SETUP STABLE BUILD]
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
* INNO SETUP RELEASE PIPELINE: Built a dedicated Windows installer using Inno Setup to create a polished, signed-ready installer experience with custom shortcuts and AppUserModelID support.
* TASKBAR BRANDING STABILITY: Verified Windows taskbar grouping with native `AppUserModelID` and bundled icon resources for consistent Ocal Browser identity instead of Electron defaults.
* INSTALLER SHORTCUT METADATA: Updated the desktop and Start menu entries to use `Ocal Browser` and the branded `icon.ico` resource across installer and uninstall flows.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[PRODUCT STABILITY & USER EXPERIENCE]
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
* STABLE CHANNEL RELEASE: Promoted the browser to stable `7.7.00`, focused on packaging reliability, tab overlay resilience, and smoother startup behavior.
* CRASH RESILIENCE: Hardened main process popup teardown paths to avoid undefined `webContents` errors during overlay close and sidebar transitions.
* UI UPDATE LANDING PAGE: Refreshed the release notes experience with cleaner version messaging and improved feature navigation links.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[FEATURE HIGHLIGHTS]
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
* INTEGRATED MEDIA MASTER: Better media popup handling with explicit browser view cleanup and faster UI response for video downloads.
* MULTITASKING EXPERIENCE: Stabilized side-by-side split browsing and overlay ordering for sidebar and web app panels.
* SECURITY & PRIVACY: Reinforced custom protocol handling and permission intercepts for a safer embedded browsing environment.
